commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the macOS Voice Wake settings view to show an unsupported-state panel below macOS 26, avoid starting preview/meter work in that state, and align the macOS Voice Wake docs with the support floor and Right Option shortcut.

PR surface: Docs +6, Other +54. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: on macOS below 26, voiceWakeSupported is false, and current main/v2026.6.5 still render the Voice Wake subcontrols and activate the preview path. The linked macOS 15 report matches that unsupported-state confusion.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because I found no narrow patch defect, and the maintainer-labeled PR belongs in normal human review/land handling.

target.title: fix(macos): clarify unsupported Voice Wake UI
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89575.

- Hide Voice Wake, recognition, test, sounds, and trigger-word controls on macOS versions below the macOS 26 Voice Wake support floor.
- Show an explicit unsupported-state panel that says Voice Wake is not supported on the current macOS version and names the macOS 26 requirement.
- Stop the Voice settings live preview/meter lifecycle from starting microphone work when Voice Wake is unsupported, while preserving saved trigger words.
- Update the macOS Voice Wake docs with the macOS 26 requirement and the current Right Option push-to-talk shortcut.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter LowCoverageViewSmokeTests --parallel`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean)

## Notes

- `pnpm format:swift` was attempted, but this local environment does not have the repo-required `swiftformat` binary installed. The commit helper completed successfully, and `git diff --check` is clean.
target.author: romneyda
